title: Reopenable Transformation Geometry
canonical_statement: Adaptive viability depends on whether recoverable future transformation remains accessible.

principle.name: reopenable_transformation_geometry
principle.statement: Adaptive persistence depends on preservation of reopenable transformation structure under recursive constraint.
principle.expanded_statement: A system remains adaptively viable whenever recoverable transformational pathways continue to remain accessible across recursive horizons.
principle.collapse_statement: Collapse occurs when reopenable transformation geometry becomes exhausted.

T_plus.name: transformational_accessibility_structure
T_plus.definition: The set of survivably reachable future transformation states accessible from a recursively indexed system state over a finite recursive horizon under admissible dynamics.
T_plus.core_question: What transformational futures are survivably reachable?

Omega_reopen.name: recoverable_reopenability_measure
Omega_reopen.definition: Measure of the recoverably reopenable subset of reachable transformational future-state structure.
Omega_reopen.core_question: Which transformational futures preserve regenerative continuity?

transformation_accessibility_structure.statement: The admissible transformation accessibility structure contains survivably reachable future transformation states accessible from the current recursively indexed state under admissible dynamics.

recoverable_subset.definition: The subset of reachable transformation states admitting recoverable continuation, regenerative reopening, or survivable recursive return pathways.

measure.interpretation: An admissible measure over recoverable transformational accessibility structure.

persistence_condition.interpretation: Adaptive persistence requires continued accessibility of recoverable transformational pathways.

viability_compression.important_note: Such compression need not immediately imply irreversible collapse if reopenable transformational continuity persists.

collapse_condition.equivalence: Collapse = loss of reopenable transformation geometry.
collapse_condition.interpretation: Irreversible accessibility collapse occurs when recoverable future transformation is no longer reopenably accessible.

interpretive_consequence.statement: Adaptive systems do not persist by maintaining static state alone.
interpretive_consequence.meaning: Adaptive persistence depends on continued accessibility of recoverable transformational pathways under recursive constraint accumulation.
interpretive_consequence.paradigm_shift: Viability is fundamentally geometric rather than purely equilibrium-based.

core_distinctions.movement_is_not_viability: A system may continue transforming while losing regenerative reopenability.
core_distinctions.reachability_is_not_reopenability: A future state may be reachable without preserving recoverable continuation.
core_distinctions.existence_is_not_adaptive_persistence: Physical or structural continuation does not imply accessible recoverable transformation.

deep_compression: Adaptive viability depends on whether recoverable future transformation remains accessible.
Persistence is preserved reopenable transformation geometry.
Collapse is loss of reopenable transformation geometry.
Reachable transformation is not the same as recoverably reopenable transformation.
A system may continue moving while losing adaptive viability.
Reopenability distinguishes viable transformation from terminal motion.
open_formalization_items: Specify measure class for \mu over \mathcal T^+_{\mathrm{recoverable}}.
Formalize regenerative reopening and survivable recursive return pathways.
one_sentence_lock: Adaptive viability depends on whether recoverable future transformation remains accessible.
